problem_title: Travel Rule exceptions delay case resolution
problem_description: Exception events often remain outside structured workflows, causing manual routing and slow escalation for compliance teams. This fragmentation hinders clear documentation and weakens traceability for audit and regulatory review.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY connects Travel Rule messages, originator and beneficiary data, provider outputs and review actions into one case management workflow that accelerates resolution while preserving audit-ready evidence.
workflow_title: From Travel Rule exceptions to case evidence
workflow_description: SFTY routes Travel Rule exceptions through automated review, case escalation, evidence logging and structured reporting within one compliance workflow for VASPs.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond message routing. Evidence-ready Travel Rule workflows
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects fragmented Travel Rule provider outputs into structured exception cases, routing decisions, audit evidence and reporting within one provider-neutral compliance operating layer.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view

authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for Travel Rule compliance?
faq_1_answer: SFTY connects Travel Rule messages, originator and beneficiary data, routing status, exceptions, cases and audit evidence into one structured compliance workflow for regulated digital asset businesses.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY handle Travel Rule exceptions?
faq_2_answer: SFTY routes, review, escalation and case creation for Travel Rule exceptions, supporting that incomplete or failed messages are monitored, investigated and resolved within a documented workflow.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve Travel Rule evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves Travel Rule decision logs, case records and supporting documents, creating an audit-ready evidence package to support compliance review and regulatory inspections.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with Travel Rule providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY works above existing Travel Rule providers to connect their message outputs, exceptions and status updates into one controlled operational layer without changing the underlying integrations.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace Travel Rule providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace Travel Rule providers but connects and structures their outputs into workflows, cases, decisions and audit evidence for better compliance management.
final_title: Connect Travel Rule exceptions into structured cases
final_description: Review how SFTY supports routing, escalation and audit-ready evidence for Travel Rule exception workflows in compliance teams.
